---
title: "alpha_v_power_simulation"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-01"
---

```{r}
set.seed(100)

reps <- 7
ntrt <- 8
nsim <- 5000

generate_rcbd <- function(reps, ntrt,
                          mu = 0,
                          sd_block = 5,
                          sd_error = 10,
                          tau_value = 5) {

  dat <- expand.grid(
    Rep = factor(1:reps),
    Rootstock = factor(1:ntrt)
  )

  beta_j <- rnorm(reps, 0, sd_block)

  tau <- c(0, rep(tau_value, ntrt - 1))

  eps <- rnorm(nrow(dat), 0, sd_error)

  rep_idx <- as.integer(dat$Rep)
  trt_idx <- as.integer(dat$Rootstock)

  dat$y <- mu +
    beta_j[rep_idx] +
    tau[trt_idx] +
    eps

  dat
}

p_values <- numeric(nsim)

for (s in 1:nsim) {

  dat <- generate_rcbd(reps, ntrt, tau_value = 5)

  fit <- aov(y ~ Rootstock + Rep, data = dat)

  p_values[s] <- summary(fit)[[1]]["Rootstock", "Pr(>F)"]
}

# Alpha values
alpha_grid <- seq(0.01, 0.10, by = 0.01)

# Power at each alpha
power <- sapply(alpha_grid, function(a) {
  mean(p_values < a)
})

# Alpha vs Power plot
plot(alpha_grid, power,
     type = "b",
     pch = 19,
     xlab = expression(alpha),
     ylab = "Estimated Power",
     main = expression("Power vs " * alpha))

# Power at alpha = 0.06
power_hat <- mean(p_values < 0.06)

power_hat
```
